news.title: Ulhasnagar civic body identifies ₹170 crore in tax linked to non-existent or wrongly recorded properties
news.para: Ulhasnagar Municipal Corporation launched 'Project Manthan' to clean its property tax database.  This initiative identified nearly Rs 170 crore in unrealizable property tax dues.  The project uncovered ghost and duplicate properties, and wrongly assessed mobile towers.  UMC staff delivered property tax bills, updating owner contact details efficiently.  This data cleaning aims for a transparent and reliable property tax system.

news.title: Navi Mumbai civic body issues notices to 295 officials over illegal constructions
news.para: Navi Mumbai Municipal Corporation issued notices to 295 officials for illegal constructions.  These notices cover deputy and assistant municipal commissioners and clerical staff.  Cidco has been made a party to the legal proceedings before the Bombay High Court.  The civic body has razed a portion of unauthorized structures since June.  Disciplinary actions will follow after explanations are received from the concerned individuals.

news.title: Residents protest over waterlogging, unfinished amenities at Zirakpur housing society
news.para: Residents of Aura Homes near Nabha Sahib protested against persistent waterlogging and lack of civic infrastructure. They claim the builder abandoned the project after selling most of the units. Promised facilities like a clubhouse and park remain incomplete despite assurances over three years. Residents urged authorities to inspect the project and ensure amenities are finished. The builder stated that 90% of the project is sold and committed to addressing grievances.

news.title: Builders’ Association of India seeks clarity on JDA registration rules in Tamil Nadu
news.para: Developers face confusion regarding Joint Development Agreement registration in Tamil Nadu. Many sub-registrar offices are insisting on JDA registration, which is not yet standardized. The Builders Association of India seeks clarification from the Inspector General of Registration. They highlighted inconsistencies and delays caused by differing practices. Clarification on legal status and fees is urgently needed for all parties involved.

news.title: Karnataka high court upholds uniform property tax for five-star hotels
news.para: Karnataka High Court upheld a uniform property tax for all five-star hotels. The court ruled these hotels form a distinct category for taxation purposes. This decision dismissed an appeal challenging the uniform levy across Bengaluru. The bench found no violation of constitutional equality principles. Limited relief was granted regarding penalty representation timelines.

news.title: Mumbai: Relief for Tardeo residents as BMC panel clears path to regularise OC-less buildings
news.para: Residents of Willingdon View Co-operative Housing Society face relief after a BMC proposal. This new guideline could regularize buildings that previously lacked an Occupation Certificate. Eligible buildings must have approved plans and valid commencement certificates for occupancy. Residential properties up to 80 square metres carpet area will qualify for this benefit. Residents now await the General Body's approval to begin the application process.

news.title: MHADA 79A amendment clears legislature, revives hope for redevelopment of 13,000 old Mumbai buildings
news.para: Maharashtra's legislature has amended the MHADA Act to revive Section 79A. This amendment addresses legal ambiguities that previously halted redevelopment efforts. The state expects this change to strengthen its Supreme Court case regarding building safety. Thousands of families reside in dangerous, ageing buildings across Mumbai city. This legislative step moves forward crucial urban renewal initiatives for residents.

news.title: DTCP orders audit of Ramprastha Primera in Gurugram after concern over balconies
news.para: Gurgaon's DTCP has ordered a structural audit for Ramprastha Primera in Sector 37D. Residents raised concerns about the group housing society's structural condition and balconies. The developer must conduct the audit and repair all balconies at their own expense. DTCP warned the developer of legal action for any negligence or failure. This directive follows a recent balcony collapse at Imperia Esfera Phase II.

news.title: All amenities provided at PMAY colony in Wathoda: Maharashtra deputy CM
news.para: Deputy Chief Minister Eknath Shinde addressed Assembly concerns about the Wathoda housing colony. He stated that basic infrastructure is complete as per the approved development plan. Allegations of missing amenities and sub-standard construction were refuted by the Deputy Chief Minister. VNIT Nagpur conducted third-party inspections, certifying all construction met quality standards. The government refuted claims of rising crime and illegal activities within the housing colony.

news.title: Odisha REAT declares builder decision to cancel flat allotment as illegal
news.para: The Odisha Real Estate Appellate Tribunal overturned a previous order regarding an apartment booking.  A builder illegally cancelled an apartment allotment after receiving buyer payments.  The tribunal directed the builder to raise a revised demand for delayed interest.  This ruling reinforces that builders cannot benefit from extensions granted to allottees.  The buyer was granted one month to make the revised payment.

news.title: About 50% of new projects under scanner have pending complaints: Gujarat RERA
news.para: GujRERA now scrutinizes developers more closely for new project registrations. Nearly half of applications reveal promoters with earlier project complaints.  Developers must resolve outstanding issues before new project approvals are granted.  This enhanced scrutiny covers buyer complaints and authority-initiated cases.  The regulator will soon display promoter histories on its public portal.

news.title: Assam cabinet approves amendment to land policy
news.para: The Assam cabinet redefined landless person eligibility for government land settlement.  Only families residing in Assam for three generations now qualify for land.  Refugees migrating before March 24, 1971, are exempt with valid proof.  Administrative approval was granted for a flyover construction project in Tezpur.  The council also approved reconstituting the Assam State Higher Education Council.

news.title: Over 11 lakh PMAY houses completed in Chhattisgarh in 2.5 years
news.para: Chhattisgarh has constructed over eleven lakh houses under Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana.  The state achieved the highest single-year completion rate nationwide for these homes.  Nearly six lakh houses were finished in the last financial year alone.  Additionally, one lakh fifty-one thousand houses were completed in the current year.  The "Mor Gaon-Mor Pani" campaign also promotes rural water conservation efforts.

news.title: Delhi court revokes bail of Nivas Promoters' director in 2023 homebuyer cheating case
news.para: A Delhi court cancelled builder Rahul Chamola's bail on Friday. He allegedly cheated homebuyers in a Greater Noida project. The court found he repeatedly violated bail conditions and abused concessions. Chamola failed to appear in court and pay costs imposed by judges. His actions demonstrated a wilful disregard for judicial orders and directions.

news.title: Keystone Realtors records ₹617 crore pre-sales in Q1 FY27
news.para: Keystone Realtors recorded pre-sales of ₹617 crore in Q1 FY27, compared to ₹1,068 crore in Q1 FY26, as there were no new launches during the quarter. Collections rose to ₹599 crore from ₹575 crore a year earlier.

news.title: New York construction scare highlights the challenges of converting offices into housing
news.para: Steel columns buckled in a New York office building conversion project. This incident highlights the complex engineering challenges of adaptive reuse projects. Engineers are working to convert older office buildings into much-needed apartments. Such conversions are crucial for addressing housing shortages in major cities. This event serves as a reminder of the intricate nature of these construction endeavors.

news.title: Goa: TCP approves 12 conversions applications totalling 2.1 hectares across five talukas
news.para: The TCP board approved twelve private applications for land conversion. These approvals allow converting green cover into settlement zones across five talukas. Applications from a sarpanch and son involved orchard land conversion at Verna. A large plot at Podocem, Sattari, was also approved for settlement. These approvals follow a recent batch of sixteen private proposals for land conversion.

news.title: Bhopal residents air grievances on civic issues in their colonies
news.para: Bhopal residents gathered to voice grievances about delayed housing possession and missing civic amenities. Many buyers felt cheated as developers shifted focus to new projects after making promises.  A multi-departmental Colony Cell has been formed to address these resident complaints. Officials interacted with affected people, addressing their concerns at the district collectorate auditorium.  Progress on resolving remaining issues will be reviewed by the administration after one week.

news.title: DLF loses ₹30 lakh after email error
news.para: A real estate agent allegedly defrauded DLF of nearly thirty lakh rupees. He exploited an internal email mix-up due to an identical name.  The accused then submitted a forged invoice with his own bank details. DLF transferred the commission amount, which was not his entitlement. He later admitted fault but failed to refund the money.

news.title: NGT directs Ghaziabad DM to prevent illegal construction in city forest
news.para: The National Green Tribunal has ordered the Ghaziabad District Magistrate to prevent illegal construction. This directive concerns the 200-acre Sai Upvan forest area, known as the city's lungs. The tribunal is hearing a case about the forest's deteriorating environmental condition. Allegations suggest the Ghaziabad Nagar Nigam is undertaking unauthorized construction within the protected zone. An interim order mandates stopping all such activities until September 16.
